mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced present value of future cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in PAYEMS (total nonfarm payroll employment) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth (>150k monthly) do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 2,000 thousand units per month indicates strong residential construction demand and consumer confidence. This demand signal predicts positive equity returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking investors price in sustained economic activity.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when HOUST > 2000 are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.10), or signal fails to outperform random baseline in more than 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth rate combined with inflation-adjusted CPI (CPIAUCSL) stability creates a stagflation-free expansion regime. When INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL growth remains below 3% annualized, equity returns are positive over 20 trading days due to real earnings growth without margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 3.0%
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in the specified regime do not exceed returns in periods of INDPRO growth with high inflation by at least 1.5%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month growth above 0.5% signals accelerating consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings. This demand acceleration drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through the market.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following RSAFS growth > 0.5% do not exceed median baseline returns by at least 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.0% signals labor market slack that constrains wage growth and inflation expectations. This slack regime produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate lower future inflation and stable monetary policy.
regime_hint: labor market slack with disinflationary bias
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5.0% are not significantly higher than returns when UNRATE ≤ 5.0% (t-test p > 0.15), or signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of elevated FEDFUNDS (>4.0%) with declining PAYEMS growth (<100k monthly) signals monetary tightening coinciding with labor market deterioration. This dual headwind produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations contract and discount rates rise simultaneously.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market softening
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in the specified dual-headwind regime do not underperform baseline by at least 1.0%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized combined with HOUST above 2,000 thousand units indicates broad-based demand expansion. This dual demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND HOUST > 2000
regime_hint: broad-based demand expansion with housing strength
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in the specified dual-demand regime do not exceed returns in single-indicator regimes by at least 1.5%, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.
